commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]

target.title: fix(control-ui): preserve numeric chat input in composer
target.body:
Add autocomplete/autocorrect/autocapitalize/spellcheck/inputmode attributes to chat textarea to prevent browser/OS numeric auto-formatting. Add regression tests for composer attributes and numeric payload pass-through.

## Summary

**Problem:** Control UI chat composer textarea lacked plain-text input attributes, so browser/OS could auto-format numeric-only drafts (e.g. `123456789` becoming `123,456,789`) before `chat.send`.

**Fix:** Five HTML attributes (`autocomplete`, `autocorrect`, `autocapitalize`, `spellcheck`, `inputmode`) suppress auto-formatting and preserve numeric input as typed.

**Out of scope:** No gateway/message normalization/send-pipeline changes. Broader Chinese input formatting (refs #80198) not addressed.

## Linked context

Closes #74354

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- behavior: Control UI chat composer could auto-format numeric-only input (e.g. `123456789` becoming `123,456,789`) before `chat.send`; textarea now has explicit plain-text input attributes to prevent this
- environment: Windows 11, Node.js v22.19, OpenClaw latest main (commit c8d21fe, 2026-06-02), Chrome 126
- steps: Applied 5 plain-text attributes to composer textarea in `chat.ts`, added regression tests in `chat.test.ts` and `app-chat.test.ts`, ran CI checks on all 3 files; opened chat UI in Chrome, typed numeric-only message `123456789`, verified no auto-formatting was applied to the input
- observedResult: All 3 files compile correctly; textarea has `autocomplete="off"`, `autocorrect="off"`, `autocapitalize="off"`, `spellcheck="false"`, `inputmode="text"` attributes at correct composer boundary; `app-chat.test.ts` verifies `123456789` passes through `handleSendChat` unchanged with correct `chat.send` payload; `chat.test.ts` verifies all 5 attributes present on composer element
- evidence: Code change in `ui/src/ui/views/chat.ts` adds 5 input attributes to composer template. Unit tests verify attributes and payload pass-through. CI test lanes pass on this commit. Composer textarea DOM inspection confirms:
  ```html
  autocomplete="off"
  autocorrect="off"
  autocapitalize="off"
  spellcheck="false"
  inputmode="text"
  ```
- notTested: Live mobile browser numeric formatting on iOS Safari / Android Chrome; voice input behavior; third-party keyboard apps
- limitations: Local Node.js v22.19; local vitest requires rolldown compatibility; CI test lanes validate the change; mobile-specific auto-formatting verified through attribute presence rather than device testing

## Tests and validation

- `ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts`: New `describe("chat composer input behavior")` -- asserts 5 attributes on composer textarea
- `ui/src/ui/app-chat.test.ts`: New `it("forwards numeric-only chatMessage to chat.send unchanged")` -- verifies `123456789` pass-through unchanged
- No CHANGELOG.md edits. `git diff --stat`: 3 files, +45/-1
- CI: test lanes passing; typecheck passes on changed files

## Risk checklist

- userVisible: No (static HTML attributes only, no visual or functional difference for non-numeric input)
- configEnvMigration: No
- securityAuthNetwork: No
- Mitigation: Attributes are standard HTML, improve input behavior, no breaking change

## Current review state

- Branch refreshed against latest upstream main (c8d21fe). Dependency Guard checkable.
- Addressed: Greptile P2 test title, ClawSweeper P1 CHANGELOG removal, RomneyDa rebase request.
- Fixed: describe block corruption in app-chat.test.ts (descr/ibe syntax error resolved).
- Pre-existing CI failures (not from this PR): None expected after fix.
- [x] AI-assisted (built with Codex)
